headline: Texas dominates the arc; LSU and Penn State shake the table
subhead: Week 7 clarified the hierarchy at the top while upending assumptions about contenders in the middle.
lead: Texas rolled through Washington with the kind of surgical efficiency that separates the pretenders from the genuine threats, reinforcing its perch atop the power poll. But the real story of the week belonged to the teams willing to upset the established order: Penn State stole a thriller from Georgia on the road, and LSU dismantled Clemson in a statement that cannot be ignored.

segments.title: Texas methodical, Texas relentless, Texas elite
segments.body: A 26-point margin does not capture the suffocation. Texas controlled the line of scrimmage from kickoff, dictating tempo and turning what could have been a competitive Pac matchup into a coronation. This is the kind of win that makes voters and analysts nod knowingly: not flashy, not desperate, just complete.

segments.title: Penn State steals a road classic from Georgia
segments.body: Georgia came in ranked third by the power poll and left with a three-point loss in a game that will replay in highlight reels for weeks. Penn State's composure down the stretch, especially in a one-score environment, suggested a team that has learned how to win close. This is the kind of road victory that either launches a season or becomes a bitter what-if.

segments.title: LSU dismantles Clemson in rout of the week
segments.body: Clemson's defense came in expecting to impose its will and instead watched LSU score 41 points with the kind of ease that suggests a talent gap. The 31-point margin tells you everything: this was not a close game that got away, but a total domination that vaults LSU into genuine contender conversation regardless of where the power poll had it.

segments.title: Ohio State and Miami trade haymakers in shootout
segments.body: A three-point game that saw 71 total points belongs in the conversation for best offensive performances of the week. Both teams scored in the 30s, both moved the ball at will, and Ohio State's ability to land the final punch in a wide-open duel suggests poise when the stakes elevate.

segments.title: Oregon's rout of USC resets the West
segments.body: A 21-point victory erases any doubt about who holds the upper hand in that pairing. Oregon's dominant performance—47 points on display—signals that the Pac has a clear leader, and USC will have work to do if it wants to remain in the playoff conversation as the season tightens.

closer: Next week's slate will test whether these rising teams can sustain momentum or whether Week 7's surprises prove fleeting.
